headline: Europe seeks to defuse Trump criticism ahead of NATO summit in Ankara
description: Disoriented by a flurry of announcements regarding the stationing of US troops on the continent, European foreign ministers sought to convince Marco Rubio of their efforts to take greater responsibility for collective defense and to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

'The very embodiment of terror': At the ICC, chilling account of crimes committed by Libyan prison official
description: Khaled Mohamed Ali El Hishri faces 17 counts of 'crimes against humanity and war crimes' allegedly committed against hundreds of detainees between 2014 and 2020 at a prison near Tripoli.

headline: Canal+ sued for alleged discrimination against anti-Bolloré signatories
description: A civil rights organization and a labor union have taken legal action after the company's CEO threatened not to work with anyone who signed a letter opposing owner Vincent Bolloré's growing control of media and cinema.

headline: South Korean Starbucks branch sparks outrage with references to historic massacre
description: Mere hours after a marketing campaign began on May 18, the anniversary of the 1980 Gwangju massacre that brutally repressed a pro-democracy movement, the operation was halted and the CEO of Starbucks Korea was fired.

headline: Heat wave in France illustrates temperature swings made more common by climate change
description: The unusual weather event France is experiencing highlights not only the early onset of high temperatures linked to climate change but also the increasing abruptness of weather shifts.

Elysée Palace searched as part of investigation into events contracts
description: The judiciary is examining how several public contracts were awarded to Shortcut Events, the company responsible in recent years for organizing major events for the president. Two investigating judges went to the palace on Thursday.

headline: France remains top destination for foreign investment as Europe declines
description: France attracted the most foreign investment in Europe in 2025, according to the EY barometer published on Thursday, despite an overall decline in the number of projects and associated jobs across the continent.

headline: Ex-PM Attal is officially running for president and wants to distance himself from Macron
description: Gabriel Attal announced his presidential bid on Friday. He's hoping to quickly build momentum in order to overtake Edouard Philippe, another former prime minister who is tipped as the favorite to represent the centrist bloc and the right.
